summary: Fix broken build tonight, generate missing devotional content, review cash deployment conservatism.

concerns: Fear & Greed at 26 — extreme fear zone

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright team, night wrap. Sunday evening, March 8th. Quick status check before we close the day. Build's unhealthy with 7 uncommitted changes in hub, Fear & Greed dropped to 26 - extreme fear territory. We've got $79K liquid cash and Dave's revenue streams are still at zero. Let's keep this tight - what needs overnight attention?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Build's broken, that's my immediate concern. Seven uncommitted changes and the latest commit message says "chore: trigger deploy" - that's usually a bad sign. I need to get in there tonight and fix whatever's causing the build failure before Monday morning. Can't have the team blocked.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Hold on Elon, before you disappear into code - this Fear & Greed at 26 is screaming opportunity. We're sitting on nearly 80 grand in cash while the market's in extreme fear. Chris, what's your read on deployment timing?

transcript.speaker: Chris
transcript.message: Dave, I hear you, but Joshua's directive is clear - keep cash liquid until we have post-launch revenue data. With 22 HOLD positions and SPY in sideways regime, I'm not seeing compelling setups that justify breaking that directive. The 2 BUY signals today are decent but not urgent.

transcript.speaker: Steve
transcript.message: We're missing the forest for the trees here. Revenue is zero because we haven't shipped. Elon, how close are we to App Store submission? That broken build better not be blocking our path to market.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Actually Steve, A-034 is progressing well - the faith journey standalone app foundation is solid. But if the hub build is broken, it could cascade into deployment issues. I need to prioritize the build fix tonight, then we can talk submission timeline.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Can we pause the technical fire drill? We're hemorrhaging opportunity cost. Six podcast episodes produced, eight on the website - there's a sync gap. Daily devotional content isn't flowing. We have 133 faith lessons and 8 dimensions, but the content machine is stalled. That's what's killing our pre-launch momentum.

transcript.speaker: Peterson
transcript.message: Alex raises a critical point. The spiritual foundation needs to be rock-solid before we launch. Those 8 faith dimensions aren't just content buckets - they're the moral architecture of the entire experience. If our devotional pipeline is broken, we're launching with a hollow core.

transcript.speaker: James
transcript.message: Everyone's talking symptoms, not root cause. Theo, do we have proper monitoring on the content pipeline? If Alex is seeing sync gaps and broken flows, that suggests our validation infrastructure is inadequate. We need telemetry on every content touchpoint.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: James, you're right. A-036 covers that telemetry implementation - it's medium priority but maybe should be higher. The question is sequencing. Do we fix the immediate build issue, or do we step back and instrument everything properly first?

transcript.speaker: Steve
transcript.message: We do both, but not at the same time. Elon fixes tonight's build fire, Alex generates the daily devotional content that's missing, and James instruments the pipeline so we don't have this conversation again next week. Dave - talk to me about the deployment strategy for that 79K. What's your recommendation?

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Here's my take - the Fear & Greed at 26 is historically a great buying opportunity, but Joshua's cash directive makes sense given zero revenue. However, we should be reviewing our defensive posture. Are we being too conservative? Chris, can you run scenarios on deploying maybe 20-30% of that cash?

transcript.speaker: Chris
transcript.message: Dave, I can model it, but the regime detection is showing sideways movement. Even in fear territory, sideways markets can stay sideways longer than we can stay patient. I'd rather wait for regime confirmation before deploying significant capital.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Can we make a decision? I've got a build to fix, and this cash deployment discussion can happen tomorrow when markets are open. The technical debt is real - broken builds block everything downstream.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Elon's right about priorities, but Peterson and I need to sync on the devotional content generation. If I'm pushing daily content based on those 8 faith dimensions, I need to know it aligns with the moral framework you've architected.

transcript.speaker: Peterson
transcript.message: Absolutely Alex. The devotional content isn't just daily inspiration - it's discipleship architecture. Each piece needs to reinforce the 8-dimensional spiritual framework. We can't treat this like generic content marketing.

transcript.speaker: James
transcript.message: Before everyone splits off into their domains, let me flag something. That Family tab is only 455 lines of code tracking 6 family members. If we're serious about the family component being core to Lever, that feels underbuilt. Steve, is that intentional minimalism or technical debt?

transcript.speaker: Steve
transcript.message: It's intentional for V1, but you're right to question it. The Family tab needs to be substantial enough to justify its place in the core app architecture. Maybe we need to expand the feature spec - but not before we ship the current build.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, converging. Tonight's priorities: Elon fixes the hub build, Alex generates the missing devotional content, Dave reviews our cash deployment defensiveness. Everything else queues for Monday morning. James, keep the telemetry implementation moving but don't block the build fix. Peterson, sync with Alex on content standards. Chris, monitor the Fear & Greed but don't deploy until we get revenue clarity.

actionItems.text: Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 455 lines)

topPriorities: Fix hub build immediately - blocking all downstream deployment
Generate daily devotional content pipeline using 8 faith dimensions
Review defensive cash posture given extreme fear market conditions

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Keep faith content as separate standalone section (not merged into Family tab)
Keep cash liquid — no deployment until post-launch revenue data
App Store submission: full current build (all 4 tabs) as-is with faith lessons. Submit immediately after merge, ship modal as day-one update.
Paper trading MVP: manual entry (6 weeks), basic free / advanced analytics premium. Integrate into current Lever app finance tab.
Launch with delayed market data for both free and premium tiers. Real-time as future ultra-premium.
Org flattened to 8 C-suite agents. No permanent sub-agents — spawn dynamically via sessions_spawn.
